{{wikidata link|Q11449252}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 宇奈岐日女神社 |画像 = [[File:Unaguhime-jinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
境内 |所在地 = {{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}湯布院町川上2220 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|15|24.79|N|131|21|56.12|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=宇奈岐日女神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}}
{{jalink|国狭槌尊|Q5966692}}
{{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見尊}}
{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|彦波瀲武鸕鷀草葺不合尊}}
{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神倭磐余彦尊}}
{{jalink|綏靖天皇|Q313321|神渟名川耳尊}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 六所宮、木綿神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月22日|Q2889}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=宇奈岐
日女神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|大鳥居と{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}]] '''宇奈岐日女神社'''（うなぐひめじんじゃ/うなきひめ-/うなぎひめ-）は、{{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 「{{jalink|六所宮}}」とも呼ばれるほか、「木綿神社（ゆふじんじゃ）」「木綿山神社（ゆふさん-）」の通称もある。 === 祭神 === 祭神は以下の6柱。「六所宮」の別称はこれら6柱を祀ることによる。 * {{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}} （くにとこたちのみこと） * {{jalink|国狭槌尊|Q5966692}} （くにさつちのみこと） * {{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見尊}} （ひこほほでみのみこと） * {{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|彦波瀲武鸕鷀草葺不合尊}} （ひこなぎさたけうがやふきあえずのみこと） * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神倭磐余彦尊}} （かむやまといわれひこのみこと、初代神武天皇） * {{jalink|綏靖天皇|Q313321|神渟名川耳尊}} （かむぬなかわみみのみこと、第2代綏靖天皇） === ウナグヒメについて === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}（{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}山頂より）
盆地は古くは湖であったが、ウナグヒメが開拓したと伝える。]] 現在の祭神は、上記のように6柱の神々である。一方『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}に記される社名は「宇奈岐日女神社」であり、かつ六国史における神階奉叙は「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれていることから、当初の祭神は「'''ウナグヒメ'''（ウナギヒメ、ウナキヒメ）」であったと考えられている『日本の神々』宇奈岐日女神社項。。「ウナグヒメ」の名について、「うなぐ」とは勾玉などの首飾りを意味するとし、こういった呪具を身につけた女首長の巫女が神に転じたと推測されている『大分県の地名』宇奈岐日女神社項。。一方、「ウナギ（鰻）」に由来するとする説もある（後述）。 このウナグヒメに関して、古くは{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}が湖であったという伝説（蹴裂伝説）がある。この中で、由布岳の神であるウナグヒメは目の前に広がる湖を見て、力持ちの大男に命じて岸辺を蹴破らせた。男が蹴破った結果水が抜けた湖は盆地となり、その跡を現在の{{jalink|大分川|Q11432686}}が流れるようになったという[http://xn--54q032alym0wz3in.com/history.html 湯布院（由布院）の歴史]（ゆふいん観光サイト）。「{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}」も参照。。大男は「道臣命」と名付けられたといい、現在も末社の蹴裂権現社に祀られている。また、湖の乾き残りが{{jalink|金鱗湖|Q11648024}}となったという伝えもある。しかしながら盆地の底にあたる地点から土器が発掘されたこと等もあり湖でないことの証拠としては、湯布院町佐土原地区のボーリング調査における地下20-30メートルからの2万3千年前の木片の発見、由布院小学校駐輪場付近からの弥生時代の土器の発見、湖ならばあるはずの{{jalink|珪藻土|Q193414}}の未発見が挙げられる（『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』（{{jalink|鹿島出版会|Q11677095}}、{{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}）p. 210）。、考古学的・地質学的には湖伝説の真偽は明らかとされていない『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』9章。。伝承の考証として、ウナグヒメを『{{jalink|豊後国風土記|Q11633698}}』にも見える「速津媛」（{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}の由来）とする説や、{{jalink|ヒメヒコ制|Q11330202}}の指摘がある。なお、類似の伝承は阿蘇地域の{{jalink|建磐龍命}}にも伝わる。 また、大分県から発見された古文書『{{jalink|上記|Q11360118|ウエツフミ}}』(鎌倉時代、初代・豊後国主{{jalink|大友能直|Q11433255}}著。一般に偽書とされる。)によると、ウナギヒメは綿花の栽培を司る神であり、このあたり一帯がユフ(木綿)と呼ばれていたことからも、かつては{{jalink|木綿|Q11457}}の栽培地であったことが推測される。 ただし、木綿(もめん)の日本伝来は8世紀であり、それ以前の古代における{{jalink|木綿 (ゆう)|Q11519320}}は普通、楮や麻などの樹木を用いた布を指す。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によれば、創祀は{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年10月であるという境内説明板。。『神社明細帳』では、景行天皇が征西のおりに当地で祭を営んだといい、同天皇3年に速津姫が勅を奉じて創祀したという伝承を伝える。 当社は由布岳の南西山麓に鎮座している。『太宰管内志』では「木綿山にます神なので木綿ノ神社ともいう」という記述があるほか、『豊後国志』でも宇奈岐日女神は由布山神であると記されており、元々は由布岳を{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}として成立した神社であると見られている。 一方、由布院盆地が古くは湖であったという伝承に基づき、ウナギ（鰻）を精霊として祀ったことに始まって、のちに由布岳の神と習合したという推測もある。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）に従五位下の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}に叙せられたという記述であり、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）には正五位下に昇叙された。これらの奉叙は、「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}には豊後国{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}に「宇奈岐日女神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは{{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}}と{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088|習合}}していたが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により現在の姿となった。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）には{{jalink|県社}}に昇格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）6月、従五位下 （『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』） - 表記は「宇奈岐比咩神」。 * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月2日、従五位上から正五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宇奈支比咩神」。 === 境内 === 境内は1万坪を超え、{{jalink|スギ|Q147388|杉}}の古木に囲まれていたが、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}3年（{{jalink|1991年|Q2062}}）の{{jalink|台風|Q140588}}で数多く倒壊等の被害を受けた。 本殿は朱塗の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。また、大鳥居が由布院の中心部に立つ（{{Coord|33|15|48.49|N|131|21|24.05|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=大鳥居}}）。 File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|本殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|参道と神門 File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === '''境内社''' * 政正社 * 厳島神 * 御年神社 File:Tadasu-sha (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|政正社 File:Itsukushima-jinja (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|厳島神 File:Otoshi-jinja (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|御年神社 '''境外末社''' [[File:Oogosha&sugi (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|thumb|180px|right|大杵社（右）と大スギ（国の天然記念物）]] * 大杵社 ** 所在地：由布市湯布院町川南（{{Coord|33|15|08.63|N|131|21|28.25|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：大杵社}}） *: 境内には国の天然記念物「大杵社の大スギ」を始めとして、多くのスギの巨木が立っている。 * 蹴裂権現社 ** 所在地：由布市湯布院町中川（{{Coord|33|14|25.61|N|131|19|32.54|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：蹴裂権現社}}） *: 蹴裂伝説を行なった権現を祀る。当地が蹴裂を行なった地という。 === 文化財 === === 国の天然記念物 === * 大杵社の大スギ - 境外末社の大杵社境内に立つ。樹齢1,000年以上、樹高38メートル。昭和9年8月9日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}湯布院町川上2220 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|九州旅客鉄道|Q498366|JR九州}}{{jalink|久大本線|Q870569}} {{jalink|由布院駅|Q8060317}} （徒歩約15分） === 脚注 === '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 大分県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）大分郡湯布院町 宇奈岐日女神社項 * 秦政博「宇奈岐日女神社」（{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}} 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 1 九州』（{{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}）） * 上田篤・田中充子『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』（{{jalink|鹿島出版会|Q11677095}}、{{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}）ISBN 978-4-306-09410-9 「9 ウナキヒメと力持ちが由布岳を蹴った」章 === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}} * {{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}} - 当社の旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}。 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うなきひめしんしや}} == Categories == Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:qqq]] 旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 六所宮、木綿神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月22日|Q2889}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=宇奈岐
日女神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|大鳥居と{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}]] '''宇奈岐日女神社'''（うなぐひめじんじゃ/うなきひめ-/うなぎひめ-）は、{{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 「{{jalink|六所宮}}」とも呼ばれるほか、「木綿神社（ゆふじんじゃ）」「木綿山神社（ゆふさん-）」の通称もある。 === 祭神 === 祭神は以下の6柱。「六所宮」の別称はこれら6柱を祀ることによる。 * {{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}} （くにとこたちのみこと） * {{jalink|国狭槌尊|Q5966692}} （くにさつちのみこと） * {{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見尊}} （ひこほほでみのみこと） * {{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|彦波瀲武鸕鷀草葺不合尊}} （ひこなぎさたけうがやふきあえずのみこと） * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神倭磐余彦尊}} （かむやまといわれひこのみこと、初代神武天皇） * {{jalink|綏靖天皇|Q313321|神渟名川耳尊}} （かむぬなかわみみのみこと、第2代綏靖天皇） === ウナグヒメについて === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}（{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}山頂より）
盆地は古くは湖であったが、ウナグヒメが開拓したと伝える。]] 現在の祭神は、上記のように6柱の神々である。一方『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}に記される社名は「宇奈岐日女神社」であり、かつ六国史における神階奉叙は「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれていることから、当初の祭神は「'''ウナグヒメ'''（ウナギヒメ、ウナキヒメ）」であったと考えられている『日本の神々』宇奈岐日女神社項。。「ウナグヒメ」の名について、「うなぐ」とは勾玉などの首飾りを意味するとし、こういった呪具を身につけた女首長の巫女が神に転じたと推測されている『大分県の地名』宇奈岐日女神社項。。一方、「ウナギ（鰻）」に由来するとする説もある（後述）。 このウナグヒメに関して、古くは{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}が湖であったという伝説（蹴裂伝説）がある。この中で、由布岳の神であるウナグヒメは目の前に広がる湖を見て、力持ちの大男に命じて岸辺を蹴破らせた。男が蹴破った結果水が抜けた湖は盆地となり、その跡を現在の{{jalink|大分川|Q11432686}}が流れるようになったという[http://xn--54q032alym0wz3in.com/history.html 湯布院（由布院）の歴史]（ゆふいん観光サイト）。「{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}」も参照。。大男は「道臣命」と名付けられたといい、現在も末社の蹴裂権現社に祀られている。また、湖の乾き残りが{{jalink|金鱗湖|Q11648024}}となったという伝えもある。しかしながら盆地の底にあたる地点から土器が発掘されたこと等もあり湖でないことの証拠としては、湯布院町佐土原地区のボーリング調査における地下20-30メートルからの2万3千年前の木片の発見、由布院小学校駐輪場付近からの弥生時代の土器の発見、湖ならばあるはずの{{jalink|珪藻土|Q193414}}の未発見が挙げられる（『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』（{{jalink|鹿島出版会|Q11677095}}、{{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}）p. 210）。、考古学的・地質学的には湖伝説の真偽は明らかとされていない『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』9章。。伝承の考証として、ウナグヒメを『{{jalink|豊後国風土記|Q11633698}}』にも見える「速津媛」（{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}の由来）とする説や、{{jalink|ヒメヒコ制|Q11330202}}の指摘がある。なお、類似の伝承は阿蘇地域の{{jalink|建磐龍命}}にも伝わる。 また、大分県から発見された古文書『{{jalink|上記|Q11360118|ウエツフミ}}』(鎌倉時代、初代・豊後国主{{jalink|大友能直|Q11433255}}著。一般に偽書とされる。)によると、ウナギヒメは綿花の栽培を司る神であり、このあたり一帯がユフ(木綿)と呼ばれていたことからも、かつては{{jalink|木綿|Q11457}}の栽培地であったことが推測される。 ただし、木綿(もめん)の日本伝来は8世紀であり、それ以前の古代における{{jalink|木綿 (ゆう)|Q11519320}}は普通、楮や麻などの樹木を用いた布を指す。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によれば、創祀は{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年10月であるという境内説明板。。『神社明細帳』では、景行天皇が征西のおりに当地で祭を営んだといい、同天皇3年に速津姫が勅を奉じて創祀したという伝承を伝える。 当社は由布岳の南西山麓に鎮座している。『太宰管内志』では「木綿山にます神なので木綿ノ神社ともいう」という記述があるほか、『豊後国志』でも宇奈岐日女神は由布山神であると記されており、元々は由布岳を{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}として成立した神社であると見られている。 一方、由布院盆地が古くは湖であったという伝承に基づき、ウナギ（鰻）を精霊として祀ったことに始まって、のちに由布岳の神と習合したという推測もある。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）に従五位下の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}に叙せられたという記述であり、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）には正五位下に昇叙された。これらの奉叙は、「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}には豊後国{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}に「宇奈岐日女神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは{{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}}と{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088|習合}}していたが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により現在の姿となった。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）には{{jalink|県社}}に昇格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）6月、従五位下 （『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』） - 表記は「宇奈岐比咩神」。 * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月2日、従五位上から正五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宇奈支比咩神」。 === 境内 === 境内は1万坪を超え、{{jalink|スギ|Q147388|杉}}の古木に囲まれていたが、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}3年（{{jalink|1991年|Q2062}}）の{{jalink|台風|Q140588}}で数多く倒壊等の被害を受けた。 本殿は朱塗の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。また、大鳥居が由布院の中心部に立つ（{{Coord|33|15|48.49|N|131|21|24.05|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=大鳥居}}）。 File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|本殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|参道と神門 File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === '''境内社''' * 政正社 * 厳島神 * 御年神社 File:Tadasu-sha (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|政正社 File:Itsukushima-jinja 所在地：由布市湯布院町川南（{{Coord|33|15|08.63|N|131|21|28.25|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：大杵社}}） *: 境内には国の天然記念物「大杵社の大スギ」を始めとして、多くのスギの巨木が立っている。 * 蹴裂権現社 ** 所在地：由布市湯布院町中川（{{Coord|33|14|25.61|N|131|19|32.54|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：蹴裂権現社}}） *: 蹴裂伝説を行なった権現を祀る。当地が蹴裂を行なった地という。 === 文化財 === === 国の天然記念物 === * 大杵社の大スギ - 境外末社の大杵社境内に立つ。樹齢1,000年以上、樹高38メートル。昭和9年8月9日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}湯布院町川上2220 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|九州旅客鉄道|Q498366|JR九州}}{{jalink|久大本線|Q870569}} ウナキヒメと力持ちが由布岳を蹴った」章 === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}} * {{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}} - 当社の旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}。 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うなきひめしんしや}} == Categories == {{wikidata link|Q11449252}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 宇奈岐日女神社 |画像 = [[File:Unaguhime-jinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
境内 |所在地 = {{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}湯布院町川上2220 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|15|24.79|N|131|21|56.12|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=宇奈岐日女神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}}
{{jalink|国狭槌尊|Q5966692}}
{{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見尊}}
{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|彦波瀲武鸕鷀草葺不合尊}}
{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神倭磐余彦尊}}
{{jalink|綏靖天皇|Q313321|神渟名川耳尊}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 六所宮、木綿神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月22日|Q2889}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=宇奈岐
日女神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|大鳥居と{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}]] '''宇奈岐日女神社'''（うなぐひめじんじゃ/うなきひめ-/うなぎひめ-）は、{{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 「{{jalink|六所宮}}」とも呼ばれるほか、「木綿神社（ゆふじんじゃ）」「木綿山神社（ゆふさん-）」の通称もある。 === 祭神 === 祭神は以下の6柱。「六所宮」の別称はこれら6柱を祀ることによる。 * {{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}} （くにとこたちのみこと） * {{jalink|国狭槌尊|Q5966692}} （くにさつちのみこと） * {{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見尊}} （ひこほほでみのみこと） * {{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|彦波瀲武鸕鷀草葺不合尊}} （ひこなぎさたけうがやふきあえずのみこと） * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神倭磐余彦尊}} （かむやまといわれひこのみこと、初代神武天皇） * {{jalink|綏靖天皇|Q313321|神渟名川耳尊}} （かむぬなかわみみのみこと、第2代綏靖天皇） === ウナグヒメについて === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}（{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}山頂より）
盆地は古くは湖であったが、ウナグヒメが開拓したと伝える。]] 現在の祭神は、上記のように6柱の神々である。一方『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}に記される社名は「宇奈岐日女神社」であり、かつ六国史における神階奉叙は「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれていることから、当初の祭神は「'''ウナグヒメ'''（ウナギヒメ、ウナキヒメ）」であったと考えられている『日本の神々』宇奈岐日女神社項。。「ウナグヒメ」の名について、「うなぐ」とは勾玉などの首飾りを意味するとし、こういった呪具を身につけた女首長の巫女が神に転じたと推測されている『大分県の地名』宇奈岐日女神社項。。一方、「ウナギ（鰻）」に由来するとする説もある（後述）。 このウナグヒメに関して、古くは{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}が湖であったという伝説（蹴裂伝説）がある。この中で、由布岳の神であるウナグヒメは目の前に広がる湖を見て、力持ちの大男に命じて岸辺を蹴破らせた。男が蹴破った結果水が抜けた湖は盆地となり、その跡を現在の{{jalink|大分川|Q11432686}}が流れるようになったという[http://xn--54q032alym0wz3in.com/history.html 湯布院（由布院）の歴史]（ゆふいん観光サイト）。「{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}」も参照。。大男は「道臣命」と名付けられたといい、現在も末社の蹴裂権現社に祀られている。また、湖の乾き残りが{{jalink|金鱗湖|Q11648024}}となったという伝えもある。しかしながら盆地の底にあたる地点から土器が発掘されたこと等もあり湖でないことの証拠としては、湯布院町佐土原地区のボーリング調査における地下20-30メートルからの2万3千年前の木片の発見、由布院小学校駐輪場付近からの弥生時代の土器の発見、湖ならばあるはずの{{jalink|珪藻土|Q193414}}の未発見が挙げられる（『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』（{{jalink|鹿島出版会|Q11677095}}、{{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}）p. 210）。、考古学的・地質学的には湖伝説の真偽は明らかとされていない『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』9章。。伝承の考証として、ウナグヒメを『{{jalink|豊後国風土記|Q11633698}}』にも見える「速津媛」（{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}の由来）とする説や、{{jalink|ヒメヒコ制|Q11330202}}の指摘がある。なお、類似の伝承は阿蘇地域の{{jalink|建磐龍命}}にも伝わる。 また、大分県から発見された古文書『{{jalink|上記|Q11360118|ウエツフミ}}』(鎌倉時代、初代・豊後国主{{jalink|大友能直|Q11433255}}著。一般に偽書とされる。)によると、ウナギヒメは綿花の栽培を司る神であり、このあたり一帯がユフ(木綿)と呼ばれていたことからも、かつては{{jalink|木綿|Q11457}}の栽培地であったことが推測される。 ただし、木綿(もめん)の日本伝来は8世紀であり、それ以前の古代における{{jalink|木綿 (ゆう)|Q11519320}}は普通、楮や麻などの樹木を用いた布を指す。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によれば、創祀は{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年10月であるという境内説明板。。『神社明細帳』では、景行天皇が征西のおりに当地で祭を営んだといい、同天皇3年に速津姫が勅を奉じて創祀したという伝承を伝える。 当社は由布岳の南西山麓に鎮座している。『太宰管内志』では「木綿山にます神なので木綿ノ神社ともいう」という記述があるほか、『豊後国志』でも宇奈岐日女神は由布山神であると記されており、元々は由布岳を{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}として成立した神社であると見られている。 一方、由布院盆地が古くは湖であったという伝承に基づき、ウナギ（鰻）を精霊として祀ったことに始まって、のちに由布岳の神と習合したという推測もある。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）に従五位下の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}に叙せられたという記述であり、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）には正五位下に昇叙された。これらの奉叙は、「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}には豊後国{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}に「宇奈岐日女神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは{{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}}と{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088|習合}}していたが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により現在の姿となった。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）には{{jalink|県社}}に昇格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）6月、従五位下 （『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』） - 表記は「宇奈岐比咩神」。 * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月2日、従五位上から正五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宇奈支比咩神」。 === 境内 === 境内は1万坪を超え、{{jalink|スギ|Q147388|杉}}の古木に囲まれていたが、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}3年（{{jalink|1991年|Q2062}}）の{{jalink|台風|Q140588}}で数多く倒壊等の被害を受けた。 本殿は朱塗の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。また、大鳥居が由布院の中心部に立つ（{{Coord|33|15|48.49|N|131|21|24.05|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=大鳥居}}）。 File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|本殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|参道と神門 File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === '''境内社''' * 政正社 * 厳島神 * 御年神社 File:Tadasu-sha (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|政正社 File:Itsukushima-jinja (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|厳島神 File:Otoshi-jinja (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|御年神社 '''境外末社''' [[File:Oogosha&sugi (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|thumb|180px|right|大杵社（右）と大スギ（国の天然記念物）]] * 大杵社 ** 所在地：由布市湯布院町川南（{{Coord|33|15|08.63|N|131|21|28.25|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：大杵社}}） *: 境内には国の天然記念物「大杵社の大スギ」を始めとして、多くのスギの巨木が立っている。 * 蹴裂権現社 ** 所在地：由布市湯布院町中川（{{Coord|33|14|25.61|N|131|19|32.54|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：蹴裂権現社}}） *: 蹴裂伝説を行なった権現を祀る。当地が蹴裂を行なった地という。 === 文化財 === === 国の天然記念物 === * 大杵社の大スギ - 境外末社の大杵社境内に立つ。樹齢1,000年以上、樹高38メートル。昭和9年8月9日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}湯布院町川上2220 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|九州旅客鉄道|Q498366|JR九州}}{{jalink|久大本線|Q870569}} {{jalink|由布院駅|Q8060317}} （徒歩約15分） === 脚注 === '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 大分県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）大分郡湯布院町 宇奈岐日女神社項 * 秦政博「宇奈岐日女神社」（{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}} 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 1 九州』（{{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}）） * 上田篤・田中充子『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』（{{jalink|鹿島出版会|Q11677095}}、{{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}）ISBN 978-4-306-09410-9 「9 ウナキヒメと力持ちが由布岳を蹴った」章 === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}} * {{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}} - 当社の旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}。 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うなきひめしんしや}} == Categories == {{wikidata link|Q11449252}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 宇奈岐日女神社 |画像 = [[File:Unaguhime-jinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
境内 |所在地 = {{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}湯布院町川上2220 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|15|24.79|N|131|21|56.12|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=宇奈岐日女神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}}
{{jalink|国狭槌尊|Q5966692}}
{{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見尊}}
{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|彦波瀲武鸕鷀草葺不合尊}}
{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神倭磐余彦尊}}
{{jalink|綏靖天皇|Q313321|神渟名川耳尊}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 六所宮、木綿神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|9月22日|Q2889}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=宇奈岐
日女神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|大鳥居と{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}]] '''宇奈岐日女神社'''（うなぐひめじんじゃ/うなきひめ-/うなぎひめ-）は、{{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 「{{jalink|六所宮}}」とも呼ばれるほか、「木綿神社（ゆふじんじゃ）」「木綿山神社（ゆふさん-）」の通称もある。 === 祭神 === 祭神は以下の6柱。「六所宮」の別称はこれら6柱を祀ることによる。 * {{jalink|国之常立神|Q478504|国常立尊}} （くにとこたちのみこと） * {{jalink|国狭槌尊|Q5966692}} （くにさつちのみこと） * {{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見尊}} （ひこほほでみのみこと） * {{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|彦波瀲武鸕鷀草葺不合尊}} （ひこなぎさたけうがやふきあえずのみこと） * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神倭磐余彦尊}} （かむやまといわれひこのみこと、初代神武天皇） * {{jalink|綏靖天皇|Q313321|神渟名川耳尊}} （かむぬなかわみみのみこと、第2代綏靖天皇） === ウナグヒメについて === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}（{{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}}山頂より）
盆地は古くは湖であったが、ウナグヒメが開拓したと伝える。]] 現在の祭神は、上記のように6柱の神々である。一方『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}に記される社名は「宇奈岐日女神社」であり、かつ六国史における神階奉叙は「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれていることから、当初の祭神は「'''ウナグヒメ'''（ウナギヒメ、ウナキヒメ）」であったと考えられている『日本の神々』宇奈岐日女神社項。。「ウナグヒメ」の名について、「うなぐ」とは勾玉などの首飾りを意味するとし、こういった呪具を身につけた女首長の巫女が神に転じたと推測されている『大分県の地名』宇奈岐日女神社項。。一方、「ウナギ（鰻）」に由来するとする説もある（後述）。 このウナグヒメに関して、古くは{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}が湖であったという伝説（蹴裂伝説）がある。この中で、由布岳の神であるウナグヒメは目の前に広がる湖を見て、力持ちの大男に命じて岸辺を蹴破らせた。男が蹴破った結果水が抜けた湖は盆地となり、その跡を現在の{{jalink|大分川|Q11432686}}が流れるようになったという[http://xn--54q032alym0wz3in.com/history.html 湯布院（由布院）の歴史]（ゆふいん観光サイト）。「{{jalink|由布院盆地|Q11577584}}」も参照。。大男は「道臣命」と名付けられたといい、現在も末社の蹴裂権現社に祀られている。また、湖の乾き残りが{{jalink|金鱗湖|Q11648024}}となったという伝えもある。しかしながら盆地の底にあたる地点から土器が発掘されたこと等もあり湖でないことの証拠としては、湯布院町佐土原地区のボーリング調査における地下20-30メートルからの2万3千年前の木片の発見、由布院小学校駐輪場付近からの弥生時代の土器の発見、湖ならばあるはずの{{jalink|珪藻土|Q193414}}の未発見が挙げられる（『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』（{{jalink|鹿島出版会|Q11677095}}、{{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}）p. 210）。、考古学的・地質学的には湖伝説の真偽は明らかとされていない『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』9章。。伝承の考証として、ウナグヒメを『{{jalink|豊後国風土記|Q11633698}}』にも見える「速津媛」（{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}の由来）とする説や、{{jalink|ヒメヒコ制|Q11330202}}の指摘がある。なお、類似の伝承は阿蘇地域の{{jalink|建磐龍命}}にも伝わる。 また、大分県から発見された古文書『{{jalink|上記|Q11360118|ウエツフミ}}』(鎌倉時代、初代・豊後国主{{jalink|大友能直|Q11433255}}著。一般に偽書とされる。)によると、ウナギヒメは綿花の栽培を司る神であり、このあたり一帯がユフ(木綿)と呼ばれていたことからも、かつては{{jalink|木綿|Q11457}}の栽培地であったことが推測される。 ただし、木綿(もめん)の日本伝来は8世紀であり、それ以前の古代における{{jalink|木綿 (ゆう)|Q11519320}}は普通、楮や麻などの樹木を用いた布を指す。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によれば、創祀は{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}12年10月であるという境内説明板。。『神社明細帳』では、景行天皇が征西のおりに当地で祭を営んだといい、同天皇3年に速津姫が勅を奉じて創祀したという伝承を伝える。 当社は由布岳の南西山麓に鎮座している。『太宰管内志』では「木綿山にます神なので木綿ノ神社ともいう」という記述があるほか、『豊後国志』でも宇奈岐日女神は由布山神であると記されており、元々は由布岳を{{jalink|神体山|Q1595289}}として成立した神社であると見られている。 一方、由布院盆地が古くは湖であったという伝承に基づき、ウナギ（鰻）を精霊として祀ったことに始まって、のちに由布岳の神と習合したという推測もある。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は{{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）に従五位下の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}に叙せられたという記述であり、{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）には正五位下に昇叙された。これらの奉叙は、「宇奈岐比咩神（宇奈支比咩神）」に対して行なわれている。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}には豊後国{{jalink|速見郡|Q1043858}}に「宇奈岐日女神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは{{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}}と{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088|習合}}していたが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}により現在の姿となった。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）には{{jalink|県社}}に昇格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|嘉祥|Q1373157}}2年（{{jalink|849年|Q23902}}）6月、従五位下 （『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』） - 表記は「宇奈岐比咩神」。 * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年（{{jalink|883年|Q30355}}）9月2日、従五位上から正五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「宇奈支比咩神」。 === 境内 === 境内は1万坪を超え、{{jalink|スギ|Q147388|杉}}の古木に囲まれていたが、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}3年（{{jalink|1991年|Q2062}}）の{{jalink|台風|Q140588}}で数多く倒壊等の被害を受けた。 本殿は朱塗の{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。また、大鳥居が由布院の中心部に立つ（{{Coord|33|15|48.49|N|131|21|24.05|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=大鳥居}}）。 File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|本殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|参道と神門 File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|鳥居 === 摂末社 === '''境内社''' * 政正社 * 厳島神 * 御年神社 File:Tadasu-sha (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|政正社 File:Itsukushima-jinja (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|厳島神 File:Otoshi-jinja (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|御年神社 '''境外末社''' [[File:Oogosha&sugi (Unaguhime-jinja).JPG|thumb|180px|right|大杵社（右）と大スギ（国の天然記念物）]] * 大杵社 ** 所在地：由布市湯布院町川南（{{Coord|33|15|08.63|N|131|21|28.25|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：大杵社}}） *: 境内には国の天然記念物「大杵社の大スギ」を始めとして、多くのスギの巨木が立っている。 * 蹴裂権現社 ** 所在地：由布市湯布院町中川（{{Coord|33|14|25.61|N|131|19|32.54|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|name=境外末社：蹴裂権現社}}） *: 蹴裂伝説を行なった権現を祀る。当地が蹴裂を行なった地という。 === 文化財 === === 国の天然記念物 === * 大杵社の大スギ - 境外末社の大杵社境内に立つ。樹齢1,000年以上、樹高38メートル。昭和9年8月9日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|大分県|Q133924}}{{jalink|由布市|Q990455}}湯布院町川上2220 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|九州旅客鉄道|Q498366|JR九州}}{{jalink|久大本線|Q870569}} {{jalink|由布院駅|Q8060317}} （徒歩約15分） === 脚注 === '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 大分県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）大分郡湯布院町 宇奈岐日女神社項 * 秦政博「宇奈岐日女神社」（{{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}} 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 1 九州』（{{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}）） * 上田篤・田中充子『蹴裂伝説と国づくり』（{{jalink|鹿島出版会|Q11677095}}、{{jalink|2011年|Q1994}}）ISBN 978-4-306-09410-9 「9 ウナキヒメと力持ちが由布岳を蹴った」章 === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{jalink|由布岳|Q705288}} * {{jalink|佛山寺|Q11385308}} - 当社の旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}。 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うなきひめしんしや}} == Categories == Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] {{wikidata link|Q11449252}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Unaguhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Unaguhime-jinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |Location = {{jalink|Oita Prefecture|Q133924}}{{jalink|Yufu City|Q990455}} 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2|33|15|24.79|N|131|21|56.12|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unaguhime Shrine}} |Deities = {{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|Q478504|Kuni no Tokotachi no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692}}
{{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ugaya Fukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Suizei|Q313321|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) 12th year of the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other names = Rokushogu, Yufu Shrine |Festival = {{jalink|September 22|Q2889}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Unaguhime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Great torii gate and {{jalink|Mount Yufu|Q705288}}]] '''Unaguhime Shrine''' (also read Unaguhime-jinja / Unakihime / Unagihime) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Yufu City|Q990455}}, {{jalink|Oita Prefecture|Q133924}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}}, it was classified as a {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. It is also called “{{jalink|Rokushogu}},” and is commonly known as “Yufu Shrine” or “Yufusan Shrine.” === Enshrined Deities === The shrine enshrines the following six deities. The alternate name “Rokushogu” derives from worshipping these six deities: * {{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|Q478504|Kuni no Tokotachi no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692}} * {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ugaya Fukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto}} (First Emperor Jimmu) * {{jalink|Emperor Suizei|Q313321|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} (Second Emperor Suizei) --- === About Unaguhime === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{jalink|Yufuin Basin|Q11577584}} (from the summit of {{jalink|Mount Yufu|Q705288}})
The basin was once a lake, said to have been developed by Unaguhime.]] Although the currently enshrined deities are the six listed above, the shrine name recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} is “Unaguhime Shrine,” and the conferment of divine ranks in the Six National Histories was made to “Unaguhime no Kami (Unagihime no Kami).” Therefore, it is believed that the original deity was '''Unaguhime''' (also read Unagihime or Unakihime). Regarding the name “Unaguhime,” one theory holds that “unagu” refers to a necklace such as magatama beads, suggesting that a female chieftain shaman wearing such ritual ornaments later became deified. Another theory derives the name from “unagi” (eel). There is an old legend concerning Unaguhime that the {{jalink|Yufuin Basin|Q11577584}} was once a lake (the “Kerisaki legend”). According to this story, Unaguhime, the deity of Mount Yufu, saw the lake spread before her and ordered a strong man to kick through its shore. When he did so, the water drained away and the lake became a basin, through which the present-day {{jalink|Oita River|Q11432686}} now flows. The strong man was named “Michinomi no Mikoto” and is still enshrined at the subsidiary Kerisaki Gongen Shrine. It is also said that the remnant of the dried lake became {{jalink|Lake Kinrin|Q11648024}}. However, archaeological and geological evidence has not confirmed the lake legend. Pottery has been excavated from the basin floor, wooden fragments dating back 23,000 years were discovered 20–30 meters underground, and no diatomaceous earth—expected if it had been a lake—has been found. As for the interpretation of the tradition, some identify Unaguhime with “Hayatsuhime,” mentioned in the {{jalink|Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11633698}}, while others point to the {{jalink|Himehiko system|Q11330202}}. A similar tradition is also told of Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto in the Aso region. According to the ancient document {{jalink|Uetsufumi|Q11360118}}, discovered in Oita Prefecture (Kamakura period, authored by {{jalink|Otomo Yoshinao|Q11433255}}, the first lord of Bungo Province, and generally considered a forgery), Unagihime was a deity governing cotton cultivation. The area was called Yufu (meaning cotton), suggesting that cotton was once cultivated there. However, cotton was introduced to Japan in the 8th century, and before that, “yū” (wooden cotton) usually referred to cloth made from tree fibers such as paper mulberry or hemp. --- === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}. The “Shrine Register” records that Emperor Keikō conducted rituals here during his western expedition, and that in the third year of his reign, Hayatsuhime founded the shrine by imperial command. The shrine stands at the southwestern foothills of Mount Yufu. Historical sources indicate that it was originally established with Mount Yufu as its sacred object of worship (shintaisan). Another theory suggests that the shrine began with worship of eels as spirits based on the lake legend and later merged with the worship of Mount Yufu’s deity. === Overview === The shrine first appears in official history in 849 (Kashō 2), when it was granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and in 883 (Gangyō 7), it was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. These ranks were conferred upon “Unaguhime no Kami.” In the mid-Heian period, the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} lists “Unaguhime Shrine” in Hayami District, Bungo Province, classifying it as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was syncretized with {{jalink|Busshōji Temple|Q11385308}} under the system of {{jalink|Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|Shinto-Buddhist syncretism}}, but following the {{jalink|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it assumed its present form. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine under the {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534}}, and in 1923 (Taishō 12), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. === Divine Ranks === * 849 (Kashō 2), 6th month: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}) – listed as “Unaguhime no Kami.” * September 2, 883 (Gangyō 7): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) – listed as “Unagihime no Kami.” --- === Shrine Precincts === The precincts exceed 10,000 tsubo and were once surrounded by ancient {{jalink|cedar trees|Q147388}}, but many were destroyed by a {{jalink|typhoon|Q140588}} in 1991 (Heisei 3). The main hall is a vermilion {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} style structure. A large torii gate also stands in central Yufuin. File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Worship hall File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|Approach and shrine gate File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|Torii gate --- === Subsidiary Shrines === '''Within the precincts''' * Tadasu Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Otoshi Shrine '''Outside the precincts''' * Ogo Shrine * Location: Kawaminami, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * The grounds contain many giant cedars, including the nationally designated natural monument “Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine,” over 1,000 years old and 38 meters tall (designated August 9, 1934). * Kerisaki Gongen Shrine * Location: Nakagawa, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * Enshrines the deity associated with the Kerisaki legend. --- === Cultural Properties === === National Natural Monument === * Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine – Located within the grounds of Ogo Shrine. Over 1,000 years old, 38 meters tall. Designated August 9, 1934. --- === Access Information === '''Address''' * 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō, {{jalink|Yufu City|Q990455}}, {{jalink|Oita Prefecture|Q133924}} '''Access''' * By train: {{jalink|Kyushu Railway Company|Q498366|JR Kyushu}} {{jalink|Kyūdai Main Line|Q870569}} – {{jalink|Yufuin Station|Q8060317}} (approx. 15 minutes on foot) --- === References === '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="note"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{jalink|Mount Yufu|Q705288}} * {{jalink|Busshōji Temple|Q11385308}} – Former {{jalink|bettō temple|Q11396755}} of this shrine. {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unaguhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11449252}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Unaguhime Shrine |Image = [[File:Unaguhime-jinja keidai.JPG|280px]]
Precincts |Location = {{jalink|Oita Prefecture|Q133924}}{{jalink|Yufu City|Q990455}} 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2|33|15|24.79|N|131|21|56.12|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Unaguhime Shrine}} |Deities = {{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|Q478504|Kuni no Tokotachi no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692}}
{{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ugaya Fukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Suizei|Q313321|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Founded = (Traditionally) 12th year of the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}} |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other names = Rokushogu, Yufu Shrine |Festival = {{jalink|September 22|Q2889}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Unaguhime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Great torii gate and {{jalink|Mount Yufu|Q705288}}]] '''Unaguhime Shrine''' (also read Unaguhime-jinja / Unakihime / Unagihime) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Yufu City|Q990455}}, {{jalink|Oita Prefecture|Q133924}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}}, it was classified as a {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. It is also called “{{jalink|Rokushogu}},” and is commonly known as “Yufu Shrine” or “Yufusan Shrine.” === Enshrined Deities === The shrine enshrines the following six deities. The alternate name “Rokushogu” derives from worshipping these six deities: * {{jalink|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|Q478504|Kuni no Tokotachi no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692}} * {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ugaya Fukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto}} (First Emperor Jimmu) * {{jalink|Emperor Suizei|Q313321|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} (Second Emperor Suizei) --- === About Unaguhime === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{jalink|Yufuin Basin|Q11577584}} (from the summit of {{jalink|Mount Yufu|Q705288}})
The basin was once a lake, said to have been developed by Unaguhime.]] Although the currently enshrined deities are the six listed above, the shrine name recorded in the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} is “Unaguhime Shrine,” and the conferment of divine ranks in the Six National Histories was made to “Unaguhime no Kami (Unagihime no Kami).” Therefore, it is believed that the original deity was '''Unaguhime''' (also read Unagihime or Unakihime). Regarding the name “Unaguhime,” one theory holds that “unagu” refers to a necklace such as magatama beads, suggesting that a female chieftain shaman wearing such ritual ornaments later became deified. Another theory derives the name from “unagi” (eel). There is an old legend concerning Unaguhime that the {{jalink|Yufuin Basin|Q11577584}} was once a lake (the “Kerisaki legend”). According to this story, Unaguhime, the deity of Mount Yufu, saw the lake spread before her and ordered a strong man to kick through its shore. When he did so, the water drained away and the lake became a basin, through which the present-day {{jalink|Oita River|Q11432686}} now flows. The strong man was named “Michinomi no Mikoto” and is still enshrined at the subsidiary Kerisaki Gongen Shrine. It is also said that the remnant of the dried lake became {{jalink|Lake Kinrin|Q11648024}}. However, archaeological and geological evidence has not confirmed the lake legend. Pottery has been excavated from the basin floor, wooden fragments dating back 23,000 years were discovered 20–30 meters underground, and no diatomaceous earth—expected if it had been a lake—has been found. As for the interpretation of the tradition, some identify Unaguhime with “Hayatsuhime,” mentioned in the {{jalink|Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11633698}}, while others point to the {{jalink|Himehiko system|Q11330202}}. A similar tradition is also told of Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto in the Aso region. According to the ancient document {{jalink|Uetsufumi|Q11360118}}, discovered in Oita Prefecture (Kamakura period, authored by {{jalink|Otomo Yoshinao|Q11433255}}, the first lord of Bungo Province, and generally considered a forgery), Unagihime was a deity governing cotton cultivation. The area was called Yufu (meaning cotton), suggesting that cotton was once cultivated there. However, cotton was introduced to Japan in the 8th century, and before that, “yū” (wooden cotton) usually referred to cloth made from tree fibers such as paper mulberry or hemp. --- === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keikō|Q329723}}. The “Shrine Register” records that Emperor Keikō conducted rituals here during his western expedition, and that in the third year of his reign, Hayatsuhime founded the shrine by imperial command. The shrine stands at the southwestern foothills of Mount Yufu. Historical sources indicate that it was originally established with Mount Yufu as its sacred object of worship (shintaisan). Another theory suggests that the shrine began with worship of eels as spirits based on the lake legend and later merged with the worship of Mount Yufu’s deity. === Overview === The shrine first appears in official history in 849 (Kashō 2), when it was granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and in 883 (Gangyō 7), it was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. These ranks were conferred upon “Unaguhime no Kami.” In the mid-Heian period, the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}} lists “Unaguhime Shrine” in Hayami District, Bungo Province, classifying it as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was syncretized with {{jalink|Busshōji Temple|Q11385308}} under the system of {{jalink|Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|Shinto-Buddhist syncretism}}, but following the {{jalink|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it assumed its present form. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine under the {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534}}, and in 1923 (Taishō 12), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. === Divine Ranks === * 849 (Kashō 2), 6th month: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}) – listed as “Unaguhime no Kami.” * September 2, 883 (Gangyō 7): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) – listed as “Unagihime no Kami.” --- === Shrine Precincts === The precincts exceed 10,000 tsubo and were once surrounded by ancient {{jalink|cedar trees|Q147388}}, but many were destroyed by a {{jalink|typhoon|Q140588}} in 1991 (Heisei 3). The main hall is a vermilion {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} style structure. A large torii gate also stands in central Yufuin. File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Worship hall File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|Approach and shrine gate File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|Torii gate --- === Subsidiary Shrines === '''Within the precincts''' * Tadasu Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Otoshi Shrine '''Outside the precincts''' * Ogo Shrine * Location: Kawaminami, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * The grounds contain many giant cedars, including the nationally designated natural monument “Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine,” over 1,000 years old and 38 meters tall (designated August 9, 1934). * Kerisaki Gongen Shrine * Location: Nakagawa, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * Enshrines the deity associated with the Kerisaki legend. --- === Cultural Properties === === National Natural Monument === * Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine – Located within the grounds of Ogo Shrine. Over 1,000 years old, 38 meters tall. Designated August 9, 1934. --- === Access Information === '''Address''' * 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō, {{jalink|Yufu City|Q990455}}, {{jalink|Oita Prefecture|Q133924}} '''Access''' * By train: {{jalink|Kyushu Railway Company|Q498366|JR Kyushu}} {{jalink|Kyūdai Main Line|Q870569}} – {{jalink|Yufuin Station|Q8060317}} (approx. 15 minutes on foot) --- === References === '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="note"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{jalink|Mount Yufu|Q705288}} * {{jalink|Busshōji Temple|Q11385308}} – Former {{jalink|bettō temple|Q11396755}} of this shrine. {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unaguhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Suizei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|lt=Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill||lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) 12th year of the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Other names = Rokushogu, Yufu Shrine |Festival = {{ill|lt=September 22|Q2889|WD=Q2889}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Unaguhime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Great torii gate and {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}}]] '''Unaguhime Shrine''' (also read Unaguhime-jinja / Unakihime / Unagihime) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Yufu City|Q990455|WD=Q990455}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system}}, it was classified as a {{ill||lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. It is also called “{{ill||lt=Rokushogu|UNKNOWN}},” and is commonly known as “Yufu Shrine” or “Yufusan Shrine.” === Enshrined Deities === The shrine enshrines the following six deities. The alternate name “Rokushogu” derives from worshipping these six deities: * {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokotachi no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} * {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ugaya Fukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto}} (First Emperor Jimmu) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Suizei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|lt=Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} (Second Emperor Suizei) --- === About Unaguhime === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{ill|lt=Yufuin Basin|Q11577584|WD=Q11577584}} (from the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}})
The basin was once a lake, said to have been developed by Unaguhime.]] Although the currently enshrined deities are the six listed above, the shrine name recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} is “Unaguhime Shrine,” and the conferment of divine ranks in the Six National Histories was made to “Unaguhime no Kami (Unagihime no Kami).” Therefore, it is believed that the original deity was '''Unaguhime''' (also read Unagihime or Unakihime). Regarding the name “Unaguhime,” one theory holds that “unagu” refers to a necklace such as magatama beads, suggesting that a female chieftain shaman wearing such ritual ornaments later became deified. Another theory derives the name from “unagi” (eel). There is an old legend concerning Unaguhime that the {{ill|lt=Yufuin Basin|Q11577584|WD=Q11577584}} was once a lake (the “Kerisaki legend”). According to this story, Unaguhime, the deity of Mount Yufu, saw the lake spread before her and ordered a strong man to kick through its shore. When he did so, the water drained away and the lake became a basin, through which the present-day {{ill|lt=Oita River|Q11432686|WD=Q11432686}} now flows. The strong man was named “Michinomi no Mikoto” and is still enshrined at the subsidiary Kerisaki Gongen Shrine. It is also said that the remnant of the dried lake became {{ill|lt=Lake Kinrin|Q11648024|WD=Q11648024}}. However, archaeological and geological evidence has not confirmed the lake legend. Pottery has been excavated from the basin floor, wooden fragments dating back 23,000 years were discovered 20–30 meters underground, and no diatomaceous earth—expected if it had been a lake—has been found. As for the interpretation of the tradition, some identify Unaguhime with “Hayatsuhime,” mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11633698|WD=Q11633698}}, while others point to the {{ill|lt=Himehiko system|Q11330202|WD=Q11330202}}. A similar tradition is also told of Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto in the Aso region. According to the ancient document {{ill|lt=Uetsufumi|Q11360118|WD=Q11360118}}, discovered in Oita Prefecture (Kamakura period, authored by {{ill|lt=Otomo Yoshinao|Q11433255|WD=Q11433255}}, the first lord of Bungo Province, and generally considered a forgery), Unagihime was a deity governing cotton cultivation. The area was called Yufu (meaning cotton), suggesting that cotton was once cultivated there. However, cotton was introduced to Japan in the 8th century, and before that, “yū” (wooden cotton) usually referred to cloth made from tree fibers such as paper mulberry or hemp. --- === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}. The “Shrine Register” records that Emperor Keikō conducted rituals here during his western expedition, and that in the third year of his reign, Hayatsuhime founded the shrine by imperial command. The shrine stands at the southwestern foothills of Mount Yufu. Historical sources indicate that it was originally established with Mount Yufu as its sacred object of worship (shintaisan). Another theory suggests that the shrine began with worship of eels as spirits based on the lake legend and later merged with the worship of Mount Yufu’s deity. === Overview === The shrine first appears in official history in 849 (Kashō 2), when it was granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and in 883 (Gangyō 7), it was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. These ranks were conferred upon “Unaguhime no Kami.” In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} lists “Unaguhime Shrine” in Hayami District, Bungo Province, classifying it as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was syncretized with {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} under the system of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinto-Buddhist syncretism}}, but following the {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it assumed its present form. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1923 (Taishō 12), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. === Divine Ranks === * 849 (Kashō 2), 6th month: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) – listed as “Unaguhime no Kami.” * September 2, 883 (Gangyō 7): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) – listed as “Unagihime no Kami.” --- === Shrine Precincts === The precincts exceed 10,000 tsubo and were once surrounded by ancient {{ill|lt=cedar trees|Q147388|WD=Q147388}}, but many were destroyed by a {{ill|lt=typhoon|Q140588|WD=Q140588}} in 1991 (Heisei 3). The main hall is a vermilion {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} style structure. A large torii gate also stands in central Yufuin. File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Worship hall File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|Approach and shrine gate File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|Torii gate --- === Subsidiary Shrines === '''Within the precincts''' * Tadasu Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Otoshi Shrine '''Outside the precincts''' * Ogo Shrine * Location: Kawaminami, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * The grounds contain many giant cedars, including the nationally designated natural monument “Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine,” over 1,000 years old and 38 meters tall (designated August 9, 1934). * Kerisaki Gongen Shrine * Location: Nakagawa, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * Enshrines the deity associated with the Kerisaki legend. --- === Cultural Properties === === National Natural Monument === * Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine – Located within the grounds of Ogo Shrine. Over 1,000 years old, 38 meters tall. Designated August 9, 1934. --- === Access Information === '''Address''' * 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō, {{ill|lt=Yufu City|Q990455|WD=Q990455}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} '''Access''' * By train: {{ill|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|Q498366|WD=Q498366|lt=JR Kyushu}} {{ill|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|Q870569|WD=Q870569}} – {{ill|lt=Yufuin Station|Q8060317|WD=Q8060317}} (approx. 15 minutes on foot) --- === References === '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="note"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}} * {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} – Former {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of this shrine. {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unaguhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Suizei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|lt=Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) 12th year of the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Other names = Rokushogu, Yufu Shrine |Festival = {{ill|lt=September 22|Q2889|WD=Q2889}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Oita|label=Unaguhime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Great torii gate and {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}}]] '''Unaguhime Shrine''' (also read Unaguhime-jinja / Unakihime / Unagihime) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Yufu City|Q990455|WD=Q990455}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system}}, it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. It is also called “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Rokushogu|UNKNOWN}},” and is commonly known as “Yufu Shrine” or “Yufusan Shrine.” === Enshrined Deities === The shrine enshrines the following six deities. The alternate name “Rokushogu” derives from worshipping these six deities: * {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokotachi no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} * {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ugaya Fukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto}} (First Emperor Jimmu) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Suizei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|lt=Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} (Second Emperor Suizei) --- === About Unaguhime === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{ill|lt=Yufuin Basin|Q11577584|WD=Q11577584}} (from the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}})
The basin was once a lake, said to have been developed by Unaguhime.]] Although the currently enshrined deities are the six listed above, the shrine name recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} is “Unaguhime Shrine,” and the conferment of divine ranks in the Six National Histories was made to “Unaguhime no Kami (Unagihime no Kami).” Therefore, it is believed that the original deity was '''Unaguhime''' (also read Unagihime or Unakihime). Regarding the name “Unaguhime,” one theory holds that “unagu” refers to a necklace such as magatama beads, suggesting that a female chieftain shaman wearing such ritual ornaments later became deified. Another theory derives the name from “unagi” (eel). There is an old legend concerning Unaguhime that the {{ill|lt=Yufuin Basin|Q11577584|WD=Q11577584}} was once a lake (the “Kerisaki legend”). According to this story, Unaguhime, the deity of Mount Yufu, saw the lake spread before her and ordered a strong man to kick through its shore. When he did so, the water drained away and the lake became a basin, through which the present-day {{ill|lt=Oita River|Q11432686|WD=Q11432686}} now flows. The strong man was named “Michinomi no Mikoto” and is still enshrined at the subsidiary Kerisaki Gongen Shrine. It is also said that the remnant of the dried lake became {{ill|lt=Lake Kinrin|Q11648024|WD=Q11648024}}. However, archaeological and geological evidence has not confirmed the lake legend. Pottery has been excavated from the basin floor, wooden fragments dating back 23,000 years were discovered 20–30 meters underground, and no diatomaceous earth—expected if it had been a lake—has been found. As for the interpretation of the tradition, some identify Unaguhime with “Hayatsuhime,” mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11633698|WD=Q11633698}}, while others point to the {{ill|lt=Himehiko system|Q11330202|WD=Q11330202}}. A similar tradition is also told of Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto in the Aso region. According to the ancient document {{ill|lt=Uetsufumi|Q11360118|WD=Q11360118}}, discovered in Oita Prefecture (Kamakura period, authored by {{ill|lt=Otomo Yoshinao|Q11433255|WD=Q11433255}}, the first lord of Bungo Province, and generally considered a forgery), Unagihime was a deity governing cotton cultivation. The area was called Yufu (meaning cotton), suggesting that cotton was once cultivated there. However, cotton was introduced to Japan in the 8th century, and before that, “yū” (wooden cotton) usually referred to cloth made from tree fibers such as paper mulberry or hemp. --- === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}. The “Shrine Register” records that Emperor Keikō conducted rituals here during his western expedition, and that in the third year of his reign, Hayatsuhime founded the shrine by imperial command. The shrine stands at the southwestern foothills of Mount Yufu. Historical sources indicate that it was originally established with Mount Yufu as its sacred object of worship (shintaisan). Another theory suggests that the shrine began with worship of eels as spirits based on the lake legend and later merged with the worship of Mount Yufu’s deity. === Overview === The shrine first appears in official history in 849 (Kashō 2), when it was granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and in 883 (Gangyō 7), it was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. These ranks were conferred upon “Unaguhime no Kami.” In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} lists “Unaguhime Shrine” in Hayami District, Bungo Province, classifying it as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was syncretized with {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} under the system of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinto-Buddhist syncretism}}, but following the {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it assumed its present form. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1923 (Taishō 12), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. === Divine Ranks === * 849 (Kashō 2), 6th month: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) – listed as “Unaguhime no Kami.” * September 2, 883 (Gangyō 7): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) – listed as “Unagihime no Kami.” --- === Shrine Precincts === The precincts exceed 10,000 tsubo and were once surrounded by ancient {{ill|lt=cedar trees|Q147388|WD=Q147388}}, but many were destroyed by a {{ill|lt=typhoon|Q140588|WD=Q140588}} in 1991 (Heisei 3). The main hall is a vermilion {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} style structure. A large torii gate also stands in central Yufuin. File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Worship hall File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|Approach and shrine gate File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|Torii gate --- === Subsidiary Shrines === '''Within the precincts''' * Tadasu Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Otoshi Shrine '''Outside the precincts''' * Ogo Shrine * Location: Kawaminami, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * The grounds contain many giant cedars, including the nationally designated natural monument “Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine,” over 1,000 years old and 38 meters tall (designated August 9, 1934). * Kerisaki Gongen Shrine * Location: Nakagawa, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * Enshrines the deity associated with the Kerisaki legend. --- === Cultural Properties === === National Natural Monument === * Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine – Located within the grounds of Ogo Shrine. Over 1,000 years old, 38 meters tall. Designated August 9, 1934. --- === Access Information === '''Address''' * 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō, {{ill|lt=Yufu City|Q990455|WD=Q990455}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} '''Access''' * By train: {{ill|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|Q498366|WD=Q498366|lt=JR Kyushu}} {{ill|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|Q870569|WD=Q870569}} – {{ill|lt=Yufuin Station|Q8060317|WD=Q8060317}} (approx. 15 minutes on foot) --- === References === '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="note"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}} * {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} – Former {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of this shrine. {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unaguhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Pottery has been excavated from the basin floor, wooden fragments dating back 23,000 years were discovered 20–30 meters underground, and no diatomaceous earth—expected if it had been a lake—has been found. As for the interpretation of the tradition, some identify Unaguhime with “Hayatsuhime,” mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11633698|WD=Q11633698}}, while others point to the {{ill|lt=Himehiko system|Q11330202|WD=Q11330202}}. A similar tradition is also told of Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto in the Aso region. According to the ancient document {{ill|lt=Uetsufumi|Q11360118|WD=Q11360118}}, discovered in Oita Prefecture (Kamakura period, authored by {{ill|lt=Otomo Yoshinao|Q11433255|WD=Q11433255}}, the first lord of Bungo Province, and generally considered a forgery), Unagihime was a deity governing cotton cultivation. The area was called Yufu (meaning cotton), suggesting that cotton was once cultivated there. However, cotton was introduced to Japan in the 8th century, and before that, “yū” (wooden cotton) usually referred to cloth made from tree fibers such as paper mulberry or hemp. --- === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}. The “Shrine Register” records that Emperor Keikō conducted rituals here during his western expedition, and that in the third year of his reign, Hayatsuhime founded the shrine by imperial command. The shrine stands at the southwestern foothills of Mount Yufu. Historical sources indicate that it was originally established with Mount Yufu as its sacred object of worship (shintaisan). Another theory suggests that the shrine began with worship of eels as spirits based on the lake legend and later merged with the worship of Mount Yufu’s deity. === Overview === The shrine first appears in official history in 849 (Kashō 2), when it was granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and in 883 (Gangyō 7), it was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. These ranks were conferred upon “Unaguhime no Kami.” In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} lists “Unaguhime Shrine” in Hayami District, Bungo Province, classifying it as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was syncretized with {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} under the system of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinto-Buddhist syncretism}}, but following the {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it assumed its present form. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1923 (Taishō 12), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. === Divine Ranks === * 849 (Kashō 2), 6th month: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) – listed as “Unaguhime no Kami.” * September 2, 883 (Gangyō 7): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) – listed as “Unagihime no Kami.” --- === Shrine Precincts === The precincts exceed 10,000 tsubo and were once surrounded by ancient {{ill|lt=cedar trees|Q147388|WD=Q147388}}, but many were destroyed by a {{ill|lt=typhoon|Q140588|WD=Q140588}} in 1991 (Heisei 3). The main hall is a vermilion {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} style structure. A large torii gate also stands in central Yufuin. File:Unaguhime-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Unaguhime-jinja haiden-2.JPG|Worship hall File:Unaguhime-jinja shinmon.JPG|Approach and shrine gate File:Unaguhime-jinja torii.JPG|Torii gate --- === Subsidiary Shrines === '''Within the precincts''' * Tadasu Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Otoshi Shrine '''Outside the precincts''' * Ogo Shrine * Location: Kawaminami, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * The grounds contain many giant cedars, including the nationally designated natural monument “Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine,” over 1,000 years old and 38 meters tall (designated August 9, 1934). * Kerisaki Gongen Shrine * Location: Nakagawa, Yufuin-chō, Yufu City * Enshrines the deity associated with the Kerisaki legend. --- === Cultural Properties === === National Natural Monument === * Great Cedar of Ogo Shrine – Located within the grounds of Ogo Shrine. Over 1,000 years old, 38 meters tall. Designated August 9, 1934. --- === Access Information === '''Address''' * 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō, {{ill|lt=Yufu City|Q990455|WD=Q990455}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} '''Access''' * By train: {{ill|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|Q498366|WD=Q498366|lt=JR Kyushu}} {{ill|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|Q870569|WD=Q870569}} – {{ill|lt=Yufuin Station|Q8060317|WD=Q8060317}} (approx. 15 minutes on foot) --- === References === '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="note"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}} * {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} – Former {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of this shrine. {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unaguhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Unaguhime-jinja ootorii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Great torii gate and {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}}]] '''Unaguhime Shrine''' (also read Unaguhime-jinja / Unakihime / Unagihime) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Yufu City|Q990455|WD=Q990455}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system}}, it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also called “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Rokushogu|1=UNKNOWN}},” and is commonly known as “Yufu Shrine” or “Yufusan Shrine.” === Enshrined Deities === The shrine enshrines the following six deities. The alternate name “Rokushogu” derives from worshipping these six deities: * {{ill|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokotachi no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|WD=Q5966692}} * {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ugaya Fukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko no Mikoto}} (First Emperor Jimmu) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Suizei|Q313321|WD=Q313321|lt=Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto}} (Second Emperor Suizei) --- === About Unaguhime === [[File:Yufuin Basin 11.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{ill|lt=Yufuin Basin|Q11577584|WD=Q11577584}} (from the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}})
The basin was once a lake, said to have been developed by Unaguhime.]] Although the currently enshrined deities are the six listed above, the shrine name recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} is “Unaguhime Shrine,” and the conferment of divine ranks in the Six National Histories was made to “Unaguhime no Kami (Unagihime no Kami).” Therefore, it is believed that the original deity was '''Unaguhime''' (also read Unagihime or Unakihime). Regarding the name “Unaguhime,” one theory holds that “unagu” refers to a necklace such as magatama beads, suggesting that a female chieftain shaman wearing such ritual ornaments later became deified. Another theory derives the name from “unagi” (eel). There is an old legend concerning Unaguhime that the {{ill|lt=Yufuin Basin|Q11577584|WD=Q11577584}} was once a lake (the “Kerisaki legend”). According to this story, Unaguhime, the deity of Mount Yufu, saw the lake spread before her and ordered a strong man to kick through its shore. When he did so, the water drained away and the lake became a basin, through which the present-day {{ill|lt=Oita River|Q11432686|WD=Q11432686}} now flows. The strong man was named “Michinomi no Mikoto” and is still enshrined at the subsidiary Kerisaki Gongen Shrine. It is also said that the remnant of the dried lake became {{ill|lt=Lake Kinrin|Q11648024|WD=Q11648024}}. However, archaeological and geological evidence has not confirmed the lake legend. Pottery has been excavated from the basin floor, wooden fragments dating back 23,000 years were discovered 20–30 meters underground, and no diatomaceous earth—expected if it had been a lake—has been found. As for the interpretation of the tradition, some identify Unaguhime with “Hayatsuhime,” mentioned in the {{ill|lt=Bungo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11633698|WD=Q11633698}}, while others point to the {{ill|lt=Himehiko system|Q11330202|WD=Q11330202}}. A similar tradition is also told of Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto in the Aso region. According to the ancient document {{ill|lt=Uetsufumi|Q11360118|WD=Q11360118}}, discovered in Oita Prefecture (Kamakura period, authored by {{ill|lt=Otomo Yoshinao|Q11433255|WD=Q11433255}}, the first lord of Bungo Province, and generally considered a forgery), Unagihime was a deity governing cotton cultivation. The area was called Yufu (meaning cotton), suggesting that cotton was once cultivated there. However, cotton was introduced to Japan in the 8th century, and before that, “yū” (wooden cotton) usually referred to cloth made from tree fibers such as paper mulberry or hemp. --- === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}. The “Shrine Register” records that Emperor Keikō conducted rituals here during his western expedition, and that in the third year of his reign, Hayatsuhime founded the shrine by imperial command. The shrine stands at the southwestern foothills of Mount Yufu. Historical sources indicate that it was originally established with Mount Yufu as its sacred object of worship (shintaisan). Another theory suggests that the shrine began with worship of eels as spirits based on the lake legend and later merged with the worship of Mount Yufu’s deity. === Overview === The shrine first appears in official history in 849 (Kashō 2), when it was granted the Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and in 883 (Gangyō 7), it was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. These ranks were conferred upon “Unaguhime no Kami.” In the mid-Heian period, the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}} lists “Unaguhime Shrine” in Hayami District, Bungo Province, classifying it as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was syncretized with {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} under the system of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinto-Buddhist syncretism}}, but following the {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it assumed its present form. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1923 (Taishō 12), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. === Divine Ranks === * 849 (Kashō 2), 6th month: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}) – listed as “Unaguhime no Kami.” * September 2, 883 (Gangyō 7): Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (recorded in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) – listed as “Unagihime no Kami.” --- === Shrine Precincts === The precincts exceed 10,000 tsubo and were once surrounded by ancient {{ill|lt=cedar trees|Q147388|WD=Q147388}}, but many were destroyed by a {{ill|lt=typhoon|Q140588|WD=Q140588}} in 1991 (Heisei 3). The main hall is a vermilion {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} style structure. A large torii gate also stands in central Yufuin. Over 1,000 years old, 38 meters tall. Designated August 9, 1934. --- === Access Information === '''Address''' * 2220 Kawakami, Yufuin-chō, {{ill|lt=Yufu City|Q990455|WD=Q990455}}, {{ill|lt=Oita Prefecture|Q133924|WD=Q133924}} '''Access''' * By train: {{ill|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|Q498366|WD=Q498366|lt=JR Kyushu}} {{ill|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|Q870569|WD=Q870569}} – {{ill|lt=Yufuin Station|Q8060317|WD=Q8060317}} (approx. 15 minutes on foot) --- === References === '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="note"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} === Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Unaguhime-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Mount Yufu|Q705288|WD=Q705288}} * {{ill|lt=Busshōji Temple|Q11385308|WD=Q11385308}} – Former {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of this shrine. {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unaguhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unaguhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bungo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:豊後国の式内社]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:由布市の建築物]] [[Category:由布市の歴史]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]
